source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/03_ADD (HAD1717MCW)/▶ FDA/1차 완료/04. Usability Engineering/Usability Validation Report (Doc.No.A-TCF-5-040(rev.1)).docx

document.title: Usability Validation Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Usability validation report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting intended use, participant testing, task completion results, and applicable usability/risk standards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports usability engineering validation for ADD Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector accessories, including HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
keyPoints: ADD is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
The device is not to be used for mammography.
Seven participants were involved in the usability test.
All nine usability task scenarios reported 100% completion and pass results.
Residual risk regarding usability is stated as minimized and acceptable.

entities.name: ADD
entities.evidence: This device (ADD) intended use is as follows:

entities.name: Abyz Digital Detector
entities.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution

entities.name: HAD1717MCW
entities.evidence: the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW

entities.name: HAD1417MCW
entities.evidence: the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient

concepts.name: General radiographic diagnosis
concepts.evidence: designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children

concepts.name: Mammography exclusion
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: Usability validation
concepts.evidence: The usability of ADD have been validated according to the usability verification & evaluation plan.

concepts.name: Task completion success rate
concepts.evidence: Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. Result: 100 % (Pass)

concepts.name: Residual usability risk
concepts.evidence: residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable

concepts.name: Usability engineering standard
concepts.evidence: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report is identified as Report No. A-TCF-5-040 Rev.1.
Revision 1 was dated MAY/25/2021 and added component HAD1717MCW.
ADD is intended to obtain diagnostic information through anatomical structure imaging using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is intended to obtain the information necessary for the diagnosis of the patient through the anatomical structure of human body obtained by a film or an image processing (workstation) process of examination using radiation exposure with cassette IP, CR or DR (Portable flat panel).

evidence.claim: ADD is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: ADD is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector accessory can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Among the accessories of the ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector can be selected between HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW.

evidence.claim: HAD1717MCW and HAD1417MCW have the same performance and usage except for size, with no usability difference stated.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same except for size, so there is no difference in 'Usability'

evidence.claim: The usability test involved seven participants.
evidence.evidenceText: A total 7 participants were involved in a usability test to ensure stable results.

evidence.claim: Participants were medical-device educated persons with a license equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.
evidence.evidenceText: Person who receive education about medical device and to have a licence equivalent to doctor or medical radiology technician.

evidence.claim: The usability task completion result was 100% pass.
evidence.evidenceText: Criteria: A total completion rate of 100 % in each usability task. Result: 100 % (Pass)

evidence.claim: Nine usability test acts were evaluated and each passed.
evidence.evidenceText: ACT #1 ACT #2 ACT #3 ACT #4 ACT #5 ACT #6 ACT #7 ACT #8 ACT #9 Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass Pass

evidence.claim: The conclusion states that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Usability verification & evaluation results and improvement action demonstrated that residual risk regarding usability is minimized and acceptable.

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2007 for medical-device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2007 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices

evidence.claim: The report references EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 for usability requirements in medical electrical equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1-6:2010 + A1:2015 Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-6: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Usability

evidence.claim: The report references EN 62366:2008 for usability engineering of medical devices.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62366:2008 Medical devices - Application of usability engineering to medical devices

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal is FDA in source metadata and path, but the document text does not explicitly mention FDA or a jurisdiction.
Acceptance criteria wording repeatedly says 'over 100%' although reported completion results are 100%.
Further Improvement section states that some problems were carried out during usability testing, but the extracted text does not provide detailed problem descriptions.
